failure_modes.text: Risk assessment becomes process theater when more deliberation is added without sharper thresholds, mitigations, or decisions.
failure_modes.source_quote: Misapplication of risk assessment often involves excessive deliberation
failure_modes.mitigation: Tie every risk review to a threshold and a decision: if no risk item triggers a plan change, mitigation, or escalation, the assessment was process theater.

failure_modes.text: The model fails when teams focus on tidy secondary variables while the main fragility drivers remain unexamined.
failure_modes.mitigation: Prioritize the primary fragility drivers: identify the 2-3 risks that would actually kill the project and focus assessment depth there instead of spreading attention evenly.

failure_modes.text: Downside governance breaks when optimism or conjunctive success thinking crowds out realistic failure paths.
failure_modes.source_quote: overestimate the probability of conjunctive events
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test the plan against realistic failure paths: require at least one scenario where conjunctive assumptions break, and verify the team has a response ready.

premortem_questions.text: What downside path is underweighted because the plan feels coherent from the inside?
What threshold or indicator would tell us this risk is becoming real rather than theoretical?
Which interdependency or unknown-unknown could break the current risk picture?

heuristics.text: Translate risk into explicit mitigations, thresholds, and monitoring instead of generic caution language.
Use systems thinking to surface interdependencies before treating risks as isolated line items.
Check whether optimism about conjunctive success is hiding the real downside shape of the plan.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on thresholded downside governance, anti-process-theater discipline, and interdependency-aware risk work.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor compliance sprawl because the raw source is stronger on risk governance and realistic downside mapping.
open_questions: The second premortem item compresses several source passages on monitoring and escalation conditions so the chunk stays operational.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on systems-thinking, constraints, resilience, trade-offs, and checklists as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy and representativeness-bias, but only clean current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit tension blocks with optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy, and that now cleanly maps into the current target-id surface.
